Set up a new Python environment or Colab notebook with Python 3.10
Install the latest versions of tensorflow and keras packages (e.g., `pip install tensorflow keras` without specifying keras version)
Import the following modules: `import tensorflow as tf` and `from keras.utils import data_utils`
Observe that the import `from keras.utils import data_utils` raises an ImportError: cannot import name 'data_utils' from 'keras.utils'
Alternatively, install the keras_cv package which triggers the import of `data_utils` from `keras.utils` internally, by running `pip install keras-cv`
Create a minimal Python script or notebook cell that imports keras_cv models weights module, e.g., `from keras_cv.models import weights` and run it to trigger the import error
Verify the exact error message is raised: `ImportError: cannot import name 'data_utils' from 'keras.utils'` referencing the file path `/usr/local/lib/python3.10/dist-packages/keras/utils/__init__.py`
Run the Fine-tuning Stable Diffusion example code from https://keras.io/examples/generative/finetune_stable_diffusion/ in the current environment without modifying keras version
Observe that the example fails at import with the same ImportError
Record the keras version installed by running `pip show keras` and note it is a version higher than 2.12.0 (e.g., 2.13.1)
Uninstall keras and reinstall keras version 2.12.0 specifically via `pip install keras==2.12.0`
Repeat the import steps: `from keras.utils import data_utils` and importing keras_cv weights module
Verify that the ImportError no longer occurs with keras 2.12.0 installed
Run the Fine-tuning Stable Diffusion example again with keras 2.12.0 and observe that it runs without import errors
Assert that the ImportError is reproducible only with keras versions newer than 2.12.0 and not with 2.12.0
